question: Install Git on your system using the package manager and verify the installation by running `git --version`
hint: On Ubuntu/Debian-based systems, use apt to install packages
validation_script:
#!/bin/bash
# Check if git is installed and returns version
if ! command -v git &> /dev/null; then
    echo "Git is not installed"
    exit 1
fi

OUTPUT=$(git --version 2>&1)
if [[ $OUTPUT == "git version"* ]]; then
    echo "Git installed successfully: $OUTPUT"
    exit 0
else
    echo "git --version did not return expected output"
    exit 1
fi

question: Configure your Git identity by setting your user name to "Lab User" using `git config`
hint: Use git config with the --global flag to set user.name
validation_script:
#!/bin/bash
# Check if user.name is configured correctly
NAME=$(git config --global user.name 2>/dev/null)

if [[ "$NAME" == "Lab User" ]]; then
    echo "user.name correctly set to: $NAME"
    exit 0
else
    echo "user.name is not set to 'Lab User'. Current value: $NAME"
    exit 1
fi

question: Configure your Git email by setting your user email to "lab@example.com" using `git config`
hint: Use git config with the --global flag to set user.email
validation_script:
#!/bin/bash
# Check if user.email is configured correctly
EMAIL=$(git config --global user.email 2>/dev/null)

if [[ "$EMAIL" == "lab@example.com" ]]; then
    echo "user.email correctly set to: $EMAIL"
    exit 0
else
    echo "user.email is not set to 'lab@example.com'. Current value: $EMAIL"
    exit 1
fi

question: Set the default branch name to "main" globally in Git configuration
hint: Use git config to set the init.defaultBranch configuration option
validation_script:
#!/bin/bash
# Check if defaultBranch is set to main
BRANCH=$(git config --global init.defaultBranch 2>/dev/null)

if [[ "$BRANCH" == "main" ]]; then
    echo "Default branch correctly set to: $BRANCH"
    exit 0
else
    echo "init.defaultBranch is not set to 'main'. Current value: $BRANCH"
    exit 1
fi

question: Create a new Git repository in /tmp/test_repo and verify that the user identity is applied by checking the commit author in a test commit
hint: Initialize a repo, make a commit, and check the author info
validation_script:
#!/bin/bash
# Create test repo and verify identity
rm -rf /tmp/test_repo
git init /tmp/test_repo
cd /tmp/test_repo
echo "test" > test.txt
git add test.txt
git commit -m "Initial commit"

# Check author of commit
AUTHOR=$(git log -1 --format="%an <%ae>" 2>/dev/null)
EXPECTED="Lab User <lab@example.com>"

if [[ "$AUTHOR" == "$EXPECTED" ]]; then
    echo "Commit author correctly shows: $AUTHOR"
    exit 0
else
    echo "Commit author incorrect. Expected: $EXPECTED, Got: $AUTHOR"
    exit 1
fi

question: Verify all your Git configuration settings are persisted by checking the global .gitconfig file
hint: The global git config is stored in ~/.gitconfig
validation_script:
#!/bin/bash
# Check that all required settings are in ~/.gitconfig
CONFIG_FILE="$HOME/.gitconfig"

if [[ ! -f "$CONFIG_FILE" ]]; then
    echo "No .gitconfig file found"
    exit 1
fi

# Check for required sections
if grep -q "name = Lab User" "$CONFIG_FILE" && grep -q "email = lab@example.com" "$CONFIG_FILE" && grep -q "defaultBranch = main" "$CONFIG_FILE"; then
    echo "All configuration properly persisted in $CONFIG_FILE"
    cat "$CONFIG_FILE"
    exit 0
else
    echo "Configuration file missing required settings"
    cat "$CONFIG_FILE"
    exit 1
fi

question: Create a new repository with default branch "main", create two commits, and verify the branch name is main (not master) using `git branch`
hint: Initialize repo, make commits, then run git branch to see the default branch name
validation_script:
#!/bin/bash
# Create repo and verify default branch name
rm -rf /tmp/branch_test
git init /tmp/branch_test
cd /tmp/branch_test
echo "file1" > file1.txt
git add file1.txt
git commit -m "First commit"
echo "file2" > file2.txt
git add file2.txt
git commit -m "Second commit"

# Check branch name
BRANCH=$(git branch 2>/dev/null)
if echo "$BRANCH" | grep -q "^\* main"; then
    echo "Default branch is correctly 'main': $BRANCH"
    exit 0
else
    echo "Default branch is NOT 'main'. Output: $BRANCH"
    exit 1
fi
